reasoning: The kernel has an arithmetic intensity of 0.00 FLOP/byte (pure copy, no computation), placing it far below the ridge point, yet DRAM bandwidth utilization is only 45.2%—well below the >50% threshold that would indicate a true memory-bound bottleneck. The dominant stall reason is Long Scoreboard at 66.7% of active cycles, which indicates warps are stalled waiting for outstanding memory requests to return. The 100% L2 hit rate combined with the offset access pattern (`in[idx + 32]`) suggests that while loads are coalesced (100% global load efficiency), the data access pattern creates long-latency dependencies in the memory subsystem that the pipeline cannot fully hide, even at 92.5% occupancy. This is the classic latency-bound signature: high scoreboard stalls with underutilized DRAM bandwidth.
